mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate over the trailing three months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month. This is an always-on trend effect present in every regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or the sign of the correlation flips in more than one of three consecutive 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month span, it signals an accelerating labor-market deterioration that historically precedes earnings downgrades and risk-off repricing, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger fires, forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the average triggered return is not statistically distinguishable from the unconditional average (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Positive industrial production growth combined with rising retail sales indicates simultaneous supply capacity utilization and demand strength, a joint condition that signals a self-reinforcing expansion phase and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. Neither series alone captures this synergistic confirmation effect.
failure_condition: Joint-positive-growth instances produce forward 20d returns statistically indistinguishable from instances where only one series is positive, or Rank IC of the joint signal is not higher than the max of the two univariate Rank ICs over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts during a monetary tightening cycle signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and a deteriorating consumer outlook, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. In easing or neutral rate regimes, the same housing decline does not carry the same negative equity signal because financing conditions are not the binding constraint.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month decline while FEDFUNDS is rising
failure_condition: During confirmed tightening cycles, triggered HOUST declines are followed by positive or flat forward 60d SPX returns in more than 50% of instances, invalidating the regime-conditional linkage.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects broad labor income generation that supports near-term consumer spending and corporate revenue, producing a small positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following week. This relationship is always-on and does not depend on any threshold or regime.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return correlation with PAYEMS MoM change is not distinguishable from zero (|Rank IC| < 0.02) across two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 4.5%, financing costs for leveraged equity positions and corporate borrowing become restrictive enough to trigger short-term de-risking flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week following the crossing.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle at restrictive levels
failure_condition: Forward 5d returns following crossings above 4.5% are positive on average with p < 0.05, or the effect disappears (|mean return| < 0.1%) across more than half of historical crossing episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained uptrend in industrial production over the trailing six months signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month. This is a continuous always-on trend-following relationship.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 6-month trend slope and forward 20d SPX return remains below 0.03 across any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation growth exceeds federal funds rate levels (implying a negative real policy rate) during a period of elevated inflation, markets anticipate forced future rate hikes and multiple compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. This interaction only manifests in high-inflation regimes; in low-inflation regimes a negative real rate reflects accommodative policy and is not penalized.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY% > FEDFUNDS AND CPIAUCSL YoY% > 4%
failure_condition: During periods meeting the trigger condition, forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the conditional mean return is not significantly different from the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).
